metadata.title: Bizarre Lineage Beginner Guide - First Session Route
metadata.description: A beginner-first Bizarre Lineage guide covering the first session checklist, common mistakes, and the progression decisions that matter early.

hero.title: Bizarre Lineage Beginner Guide for a clean first session
hero.description: This page is written for players who want a usable route, not a lore dump. Start with the checklist, avoid common early waste, and branch into the pages that answer your next decision.

introduce.title: Fast start checklist
introduce.description: The first-time player experience should be broken into short, low-risk actions. Each step below exists to reduce wasted time in the opening stretch.

items.title: Check codes before grinding
items.description: Free rewards can change your early route, so the Codes page is often the fastest first stop.

items.title: Learn the current progression loop
items.description: Do not assume old routes still hold after patches. Use the current MVP guide as the baseline and update details after in-game checks.

items.title: Pick early goals, not long-term fantasies
items.description: The first session should focus on consistency, survivability, and learning the game loop instead of chasing rare outcomes immediately.

items.title: Use the tier list as context
items.description: A tier list helps you decide what is strong, but the beginner route still needs to match what you can realistically access early.

benefits.title: What beginners usually get wrong
benefits.description: Most early friction comes from committing too soon, ignoring current information, or copying endgame advice too early.

items.title: Grinding without checking free rewards
items.description: If a code grants resources or rerolls, it can change your first decisions immediately.

items.title: Copying advanced builds too early
items.description: A beginner route should emphasize stability and clarity before it emphasizes high-ceiling optimization.

items.title: Playing without a next-page handoff
items.description: A good guide points you toward the codes page, the tier list, and a stand page when each one becomes useful.

features.title: First-session route
features.description: This is the action-oriented structure the full guide should keep even after the content becomes more detailed.

items.title: Step 1: Claim any currently verified rewards
items.description: Open the Codes page before committing time to farming if free resources are available.

items.title: Step 2: Stabilize your early gameplay loop
items.description: Learn the basic quest or farming cycle that keeps progress moving instead of chasing rare outcomes immediately.

items.title: Step 3: Use the tier list for direction, not blind copying
items.description: The ranking page should help you compare goals, but your route still depends on what you can actually access now.

items.title: Step 4: Research one stand page before investing heavily
items.description: Read the sample Star Platinum page to see how the wiki frames strengths, weaknesses, and time investment.

items.title: Step 5: Recheck assumptions after updates
items.description: Roblox games shift quickly. Early routes should always note when they need a fresh pass.

items.question: What page should a brand-new player open first?
items.answer: Open the Codes page first for any free rewards, then return here for the first-session route.

items.question: Should beginners follow the tier list immediately?
items.answer: Use it as context, but do not over-commit to advanced rankings before you understand the early gameplay loop and what resources you actually have.

items.question: Why is the guide short instead of exhaustive?
items.answer: The MVP is built around fast decisions. It keeps the opening route readable and hands off to more specific pages when needed.

cta.title: Ready for the next choice?
cta.description: Move into the Tier List for meta context or open the Star Platinum page to see the stand template in action.
